summary: Full backend + frontend regression for Project Nexus. All 20 backend pytest cases pass against the public preview URL, exercising cookie-based JWT auth (CORS allow-list), the four refactored batch-fetch endpoints (projects, forum with comment_count aggregation, conversations, comments), and the new /api/profile avatar field. Playwright UI test executes the full user journey (login → reload persistence → dashboard → projects filter+create+apply → forum comment/upvote/community filter → discover→message → profile avatar customization with all 3 modes → AI Match → Opportunities → logout). Everything works end-to-end; a single minor UX nit found on logout redirect.

ui_bugs.issue: After logout, URL ends on /login instead of / (landing). Race condition: setUser(false) triggers Protected wrapper's <Navigate to='/login'/> synchronously on re-render before nav('/') runs in handleLogout. User is still successfully logged out — purely a routing UX nit. Fix: call nav('/') first, or make the Protected wrapper redirect to '/' instead of '/login'.

action_items: OPTIONAL/LOW: Fix logout landing redirect so user ends on '/' (landing) instead of '/login' after logging out (adjust order in AppShell.handleLogout or update the Protected fallback).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py mixes the whole app in a single 489-line file — as the app grows, split into routers (auth, projects, forum, messages, match) and a services/db module. Not urgent but recommended.
Pytest warning: TestProjects.test_create_project_and_appears_in_list uses `return created` — should not return values from pytest tests (only assert). Harmless but cleanup recommended.
AI match endpoint hardcodes model 'gpt-5.4-mini' inside call — consider moving to env for easier swapping.
seed() only runs when db.users has zero students; if the seed_data changes (new projects/forum posts), existing dbs won't pick up updates. Consider a version tag or upsert-based seeding.

Profile.js reads user.reputation only from AuthContext user object, so the sidebar preview shows reputation from the initial login, not the freshly-saved data. Minor since reputation isn't editable on the profile page.

success_rate.frontend: 100% of critical flows passing; 1 low-priority UX nit

test_credentials: alice@lincolnhs.edu / password123, bob@westfield.edu / password123 — all seeded students share password123
seed_data_creation: Test used seeded data + created TEST_ prefixed projects/posts/messages during runs. No cleanup done (safe — clearly namespaced with TEST or UI Test prefixes).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — run with `pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v`. It uses REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env so it exercises the public ingress + real cookies. All 20 tests pass in ~6s. UI tested via Playwright against https://teammate-discovery.preview.emergentagent.com — cookies (secure, samesite=none) work correctly across the preview origin because CORS_ORIGINS env includes it.

rca_of_the_issue.logout_redirect: In /app/frontend/src/components/AppShell.js `handleLogout`, after `await logout()` sets user=false in AuthContext, React re-renders. The Protected route wrapper in App.js line 28 sees !user and does <Navigate to='/login' replace/>. This happens BEFORE the subsequent `nav('/')` call in handleLogout executes, so the final URL is /login instead of /. Fix: either (a) `nav('/')` before `setUser(false)` (call nav first, then await logout in background), (b) change Protected redirect target to '/', or (c) move the navigation inside the AuthContext.logout itself. Not blocking — user is fully logged out and can reach landing from /login via the header link.
